commands.review_summary:
Adds a draft RFC defining opt-in cloud-serializable SQLite durability semantics around snapshots, deltas, restore verification, writer ownership, and provider-owned artifact storage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds RFC text and does not report a runtime bug. I checked the diff, current RFC set, and repository RFC lifecycle instead of running a behavior reproduction.

target.title: RFC 0013: Cloud-Serializable SQLite State
target.body:
## Summary

- Proposes RFC 0013, Cloud-Serializable SQLite State.
- Keeps SQLite as the hot local runtime database while defining managed durability semantics around verified snapshots, deltas, restore, and writer ownership.
- Avoids choosing PostgreSQL or any replacement backend in this RFC.
- Frames cloud/object storage as artifact storage for snapshots and deltas, not live database files.

## Scope

This is an RFC only. It does not change OpenClaw runtime behavior, add storage configuration, or implement backup/restore commands.

## Validation

- Draft follows `rfcs/0000-template.md` shape.
- Related issue context checked: openclaw/openclaw#90370, #94675, and #88838.

## RFC Lifecycle

- Status: draft
- Implementation issue: not created
- Maintainer discussion thread: pending
